subject: Statistics
title: Player Tracking Data
description: A quick win: understand Player Tracking Data.

hook.text: 25 times per second, cameras track every player and the ball on the field. That's 3.6 million data points per game.

buildup.text: Tracking data captures position, speed, acceleration, and distance for every player every moment.

discovery.text: It reveals things invisible to the eye: defensive positioning, off-ball movement, and spatial pressure.

twist.text: The data is so vast that most teams can't use it fully. Having data and having insight are different things.

climax.text: The teams that turn tracking data into decisions — not just dashboards — gain the competitive edge.

punchline.text: Millions of data points per game. Insight is the bottleneck.

quiz.question: What is the main challenge with player tracking data?
options: The data volume is enormous — turning it into actionable insight is harder than collecting it
The cameras are too expensive for most teams
Players don't consent to being tracked
